claim: Tradition/ability design deliberately normalizes every tradition to an equal affinity budget for anti-dominance, which narrows build-time differentiation exactly where the corpus wants meaningful choice.
cite: designs/scene/combat_engine_v1/tradition_decomposition_v1.md (WS-4 dissolution: equal affinity budget, 6.8pp win-rate spread measured)

claim: Successive biomechanical-fidelity passes (5-tuple mode-select return, per-element percussion, grip-reach oscillator fix) are eroding Q-elegant's 'restatable after one reading' bar even though each individual increment is well-gated.

From mass_battle_v30.md/integration_v30.md state alone, there is no field for a named general's or faction's goal/motive, so the dramatic-legibility question "what does each named actor want" cannot be answered from this subsystem in isolation.
cite: mass_battle_v30.md (whole doc, no motive/goal field); scale_transitions_v30.md §3.7-§3.8

claim: Even where consumed, the §3.8 Scene→Mass bonus (+1D one unit, 1 turn, or a free Reform) is a small, single-turn modifier that is mechanically negligible against the Command-dominance axiom it would need to offset, undercutting the narrative payoff of winning the antecedent scene.

The ED-1019+ per-sub-unit continuous (float) Morale/aggregate/cascade model is mechanically deep but has no player-facing surface besides the single Army Morale composite; if the videogame HUD doesn't expose per-sub-unit state, the depth is invisible and if it does, the composite's legibility value is undercut by exposing raw floats.

The rebuilt sim/personal/contest/ package has zero threadwork/Leap/Weaving code despite §9.3/9.4/9.4b being well-specified in the design doc; the archived 'clean' threadwork verdict predates this rebuild and assessed prose, not this kernel.

The Stage-3 Adjudicator Armature (continuous 4-axis dot product, delta-sigma leverage, partial-reveal Appraise bands) is a strong genuine-uncertainty bet mechanic, notably more sophisticated than the sibling npc_behavior categorical Resonant-Style +1D it deliberately does not duplicate.
The +2D Recall citation bonus is available in either genre with no real opportunity cost besides prep, making it a near-always-take checkbox rather than a bet in per-exchange (Formal Contest) form.
All 6 canonical factions default to single-root Cascade, collapsing the many-actor conviction-blending mechanic into one leader-stat lookup at scenario init.
Institutional Consolidation passive recovery grants Stability+1/Accord+1 for doing nothing a season, a candidate turtle-and-wait degenerate line.
settlement_adjacency_v30.md's own worked hub/edge examples cite pre-PP-726 settlement IDs that contradict the actual canonical adjacency data, which already uses the post-renumbering IDs.

threadwork_v30.md's own ST-TW-01 design note flags a candidate degenerate line (free Object-scale Weaving in protected combat rounds) and cites 'ED-046' for adjudication, but ED-046 does not exist anywhere in canon/editorial_ledger.jsonl — never registered, never resolved.
source: dossier:threadwork (Coherence/MS-RS/Calamity risk economy, Knots, calamity radiation, horizontal integration spec)

claim: threadwork_v30.md §2.4 retains the full STRUCK pre-PP-622 single-axis Ob tables inline for Weave/Pull/Lock/Dissolve alongside canonical-pointer footnotes, doubling the live-vs-history parse burden against the Q-elegant 'restatable after one reading' bar.
cite: threadwork_v30.md §2.4 (four Ob-by-scale tables, each footnoted pre-PP-622)
source: dossier:threadwork (Coherence/MS-RS/Calamity risk economy, Knots, calamity radiation, horizontal integration spec)

claim: The §12 Canon Compliance table addresses only 4 of 15 P-numbers and mostly with 'not directly invoked' rather than demonstrated compliance, leaving P-03/04/05/08/10/11/13/15 unaddressed even as N/A.

Only Varfell and Wardens Priority Trees have Thread-state-conditioned rows; Church, Crown, Hafenmark, Guilds, Löwenritter, and RM trees have none, despite P-01 inseparability implying Thread events in their territories should shape institutional posture.
The file carries four disagreeing status markers (CANONICAL x2, PROVISIONAL x3 incl. footer) and CURRENT.md lists it with no disambiguating annotation, unlike sibling rows.
scene.thread_operation is a registered Key type but its payload is explicitly unratified stub content, so threadwork's substrate representation is present-but-thin rather than fully specified.
GD-1 canon text names the threshold track 'Political Stability'; no design doc defines that name — it is Turmoil everywhere else, aliased only via an unlabeled sim comment.
source: dossier:victory_strategic (Peninsular Sovereignty / peninsular_strain Accord-Turmoil / GD-3 insurgency pipeline / player_agency strategic layer)

claim: victory_v30.md header still cites 'pending Varfell Path B user decision (ED-311)' as open, though GD-1 already struck all Southernmost Dominion/Path B variants.
source: dossier:victory_strategic (Peninsular Sovereignty / peninsular_strain Accord-Turmoil / GD-3 insurgency pipeline / player_agency strategic layer)

claim: 53% of all 957 open items are tagged P1, dominated by cosmetic naming-collision flags rather than load-bearing decisions, so the severity tag fails to triage — a designer cannot use 'is this P1' to decide what to work on next.
source: dossier:registers_graphs (SHAPE lane: mechanics_index.yaml, canonical_sources.yaml, supersession_register.yaml, tools/observability/DECISIONS.md, tests/coverage_matrix.md)

claim: The file's own title ('Weapon System v2 (Active)') no longer describes its content, which is entirely mass_battle engine coverage from 2026-06-15 onward — a shape artifact whose header has drifted from what it tracks.
source: dossier:registers_graphs (SHAPE lane: mechanics_index.yaml, canonical_sources.yaml, supersession_register.yaml, tools/observability/DECISIONS.md, tests/coverage_matrix.md)

claim: Two registries with overlapping scope (mechanic-list vs module-list) use divergent schemas — module_contracts.yaml tracks doc:null/[ASSUMPTION]/orphan status, mechanics_index.yaml has no equivalent staleness/orphan field at all — and neither cross-references the other.
cite: canon/mechanics_index.yaml (categories section, no gap-tracking field) vs designs/audit/2026-07-04-ners-qualitative-audit/00_grounding/02_interdependency_map.md §(b)
source: dossier:registers_graphs (SHAPE lane: mechanics_index.yaml, canonical_sources.yaml, supersession_register.yaml, tools/observability/DECISIONS.md, tests/coverage_matrix.md)

claim: The 'affinity budget' that config and the tradition-decomposition cite as balancing the imposition gate does not exist in code; ability_primitives enforces no budget and no tradition-gate, so future ability exposure permits unconstrained cross-tradition multiplicative stacking.

The §A.10 War-scale '−2/op' Coherence cost contradicts the adjacent '−1 per operation cap, no additional surcharge' note and PP-501's 1/turn worked example; simultaneously, keying substrate cost to battle scale lets a player split into Company-scale engagements for 0-cost thread ops, compounding PP-508 split-dominance into a double-dominant line.
cite: mass_battle_v30.md §A.10 scale table + cap note + PP-501; §A.3 scale-by-force-size

claim: Walls (+3 DR, no flanking) × Shield Wall (+2D Def blanket) × the Phase-7 idle-Morale terrain waiver produces a cost-free defensive turtle whose besieged decision tree collapses to a single strictly-optimal 'hold' with no morale downside — N-grounded as siege realism, but the idle-waiver removes the 'sortie or rot' tension that would keep it a real choice.
cite: mass_battle_v30.md §A.9 Walls, §A.6/PP-500 Shield Wall, §A.7 Phase-7 idle exception; §A.14b Campaign Supply

claim: §3.8 grants three uncapped, cost-free, distinct pre-battle bonus channels (Social→+1D Command, Investigation→+1D Volley, Combat→free Reform) with no per-battle feeder-scene cap, degenerating the scene-vs-battle tempo choice into a mandatory turn-1 farming ritual any optimal player performs — bounded but non-dominant-eroding.

Saturating size-weighted Mandate lets a faction reach Mandate 5 by developing one metropolis province, decoupling institutional power (and Parliament votes) from territorial breadth and making the hegemon>=5 half of the Line-1 domination clause nearly free — largely intended/N-grounded, so friction not flatten.

Knot Anchoring Scenes have no frequency cap (knots §8.2), so a practitioner refills +1 Coherence per Close Knot per cycle (up to floor(Bonds/2)+1 Knots), making the intended one-way descent to Rendering Crisis opt-in; bounded only by Knot strain budget.

Three live mechanics serve no throughline: territory temperament α/β (zero mechanical consumption anywhere), Institutional Consolidation passive recovery (an anti-М-1 rest state), and the +2D Recall citation (an anti-М-6 near-costless checkbox) — mechanics without a why, the inverse of the orphan-throughline gap.

In names_index only world.solmund is enforce:block; every attribute, faction/settlement stat, clock (including MS/RS) and mechanic rename is enforce:warn, so the Godot-load-bearing definition names have no hard gate and drift persists by construction.
cite: references/names_index.yaml (enforce fields across all non-solmund entries)

claim: No steering surface defines a playable-season or first end-to-end playable slice; the conversion strategy sequences module ports but names no integration milestone, so 'progress toward Godot' has no legible finish line to sequence against.
cite: designs/audit/2026-06-10-godot-conversion-strategy/godot_conversion_strategy_v1.md §VI (port sequencing, no milestone); references/roadmap_state.yaml (phase table, no Godot milestone)

claim: Only 2 of 9 faction Priority Trees (Varfell §8.5, Wardens §8.10) carry any Thread-conditioned row; Church/Crown/Hafenmark/Guilds/Löwenritter/RM have none, so institutional posture ignores Thread events in their own territory — arbitrary, not scaled to thread-exposure; wire a Thread-state row per tree.

The Scene→Mass win-bonus table covers Social/Investigation/Combat wins only, with no Thread-scene channel, despite thread being the densest personal-scale action (§A.10) — the one action with no cross-scale up-transition.
